description: Create art using recursion — fractals, trees, or nested shapes that repeat at different scales.
Use noise-driven vector fields to guide particles, lines, or color across the canvas.
Explore rotational or reflective symmetry. Tile the plane with repeating patterns.
Implement rule-based systems where cells evolve based on their neighbors.
Focus on color theory. Create a piece that explores complementary, analogous, or gradient palettes.
Simulate gravity, springs, collisions, or fluid dynamics in a visual piece.
Use letters, words, or text as the primary visual element in your composition.
Create visuals that respond to audio input or generated sound patterns.
Express maximum impact with minimum elements. Less is more.
Simulate natural growth patterns: branching, diffusion, or biological morphogenesis.
Create patterns that trick the eye — moire, impossible geometry, or motion illusions.
Visualize a dataset in an artistic way. Transform numbers into beauty.
